cards.def: To corrupt, debase, or make impure by adding inferior materials or elements.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Adulterate milk with water"
cards.context: ETS often contrasts this word with pure or unblemished states, particularly in the context of scientific samples or culinary items.
cards.vis: An adult enters a pristine lab and alters a pure potion by dumping cheap, murky water into it, completely ruining the formula.

cards.def: To publicly recommend, support, or urge for a cause or policy.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Advocate for environmental policy reforms"
cards.context: Used in sentence completions to identify characters or authors who champion progressive reforms or specific philosophical stances.
cards.vis: A passionate speaker stands on a stage shouting into a megaphone to add a massive glowing vote into a ballot box for a green planet.

cards.def: To increase the power, status, or wealth of an individual or nation; to make something appear greater.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Aggrandize a personal legacy through false claims"
cards.context: Frequently used to describe megalomaniacal rulers or politicians who distort facts to exaggerate their achievements.
cards.vis: A tiny, insecure king blows frantically into a balloon version of himself until it inflates to a grand size, towering over the court.

cards.def: Brisk, cheerful, and eager readiness.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Accept the job offer with alacrity"
cards.context: Typically serves as a key signal word for someone cooperating willingly or tackling a difficult task with unexpected enthusiasm.
cards.vis: A hyper-energetic worker jolted by electricity zips around the office at lightning speed, smiling wildly while filing papers.

cards.def: Having mixed, contradictory, or fluctuating feelings or ideas about something or someone.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Feel ambivalent about relocating to a new city"
cards.context: Appears when a character stands directly between two choices, unable to commit because both options have strong pros and cons.
cards.vis: A confused person weighing a massive gold bar in their left hand and a massive diamond in their right, groaning in agonizing indecision.

cards.def: To make something bad or unsatisfactory better.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Ameliorate harsh living conditions"
cards.context: Often used in passages discussing socioeconomic reforms, medical breakthroughs, or mitigation of environmental crises.
cards.vis: A sick girl named Amelia eats a glowing, magical medicine that instantly makes her pale, sickly face flush with healthy, vibrant color.

cards.def: Open and responsive to suggestion; easily persuaded or controlled.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "An administration amenable to policy changes"
cards.context: Indicates flexibility and willingness to cooperate, often contrasted with obstinate or dogmatic individuals.
cards.vis: A judge nods cheerfully, saying 'Amen' to every single suggestion thrown at him by the jury, eager to agree.

cards.def: Belonging or appropriate to a period other than that in which it exists, especially a thing that is conspicuously old-fashioned.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "An anachronistic typewriter in a modern tech office"
cards.context: Used in text completions detailing chronological errors, historical inaccuracies, or out-of-date social customs.
cards.mnem: Anna's chrome watch in history class.
cards.vis: A medieval knight pulling out a sleek, modern digital chrome Apple Watch while riding a horse in the middle of a 12th-century battle.

cards.def: Showing a willingness to take surprisingly bold risks; or showing an impudent lack of respect.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "An audacious plan to scale the sheer cliff face"
cards.context: Can have a positive connotation (bravery) or negative connotation (insolence). The prompt context will tip the scale.
cards.vis: A daring bank robber boldly walks into a high-security vault, grabs cash in broad daylight, and winks at the security camera.

cards.def: Having or showing an extreme greed for wealth or material gain.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "An avaricious corporate executive hoarding bonuses"
cards.context: Used to characterize villains, corrupt merchants, or ruthless empires driven solely by monetary profit.
cards.mnem: Have a rice issue (hoarding all the rice).
cards.vis: A greedy miser diving Scrooge-McDuck style into a massive mountain of golden rice grains, swimming and refusing to share a single grain.

cards.def: So lacking in originality as to be obvious and boring.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Banal television sitcoms with predictable plotlines"
cards.context: Often serves as the negative contrast to innovative, avant-garde, or groundbreaking artistic achievements.
cards.vis: A judge hitting a gavel to 'ban all' boring, predictable romance novels, tossing them into a giant dumpster of unoriginality.

cards.def: Gentle and kindly; (of a disease) not harmful in effect.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A benign tumor that requires no surgery"
cards.context: Appears when describing gentle rulers, harmless situations, or medical diagnoses that bring relief to characters.
cards.vis: A sweet, gentle 9-year-old child handing a delicate flower to a giant, ferocious monster, instantly calming it down.

cards.def: Bold and without shame; audacious or insolent.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A brazen lie told directly to the investigator"
cards.context: Differs from audacious by leaning much more heavily into a negative, shameless, or disrespectful tone.
cards.mnem: Brass instrument played loudly in a library.
cards.vis: A student standing up in a dead-silent university library, blasting a shiny brass trumpet directly into the librarian's face without blinking.

cards.def: The making of false and defamatory statements about someone in order to damage their reputation; slander.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A political campaign fueled by malicious calumny"
cards.context: Often paired in Sentence Equivalence with words like 'slander' or 'defamation' within toxic political or social climates.
cards.vis: A corrupt journalist printing a massive, glowing headline that reads an absolute LIE in a newspaper column to destroy a hero's life.

cards.def: Truthful and straightforward; frank or unreserved.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A candid discussion about the company's financial failures"
cards.context: Used to describe refreshing honesty in a setting filled with deceit, hypocrisy, or euphemisms.
cards.vis: A politician throws away his scripted speech, steps out from behind the podium, and talks with raw, pure, unfiltered honesty to the crowd.

cards.def: To reprimand someone severely; admonish or chastise harshly.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Castigate the employee for a critical security breach"
cards.context: Look for severe institutional backlash, furious critics, or parents severely reprimanding wayward children.
cards.vis: A furious warden screaming angrily while slamming a criminal's cage gate shut, berating him with ferocious insults.

cards.def: Sarcastic in a scathing and bitter way; or able to burn or corrode organic tissue by chemical action.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Caustic remarks that offended everyone at the dinner table"
cards.context: Appears frequently to describe critical reviews, bitter insults, or sharp tongues that destroy social cohesion.
cards.vis: Words flying out of a cynic's mouth literalized as green acid droplets that burn and melt a wooden walking stick in half.

cards.def: To interpret or understand the meaning of a word, action, or remark in a particular way.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Her silence was construed as an admission of guilt"
cards.context: Often appears in the passive voice ('was construed as') indicating how an ambiguous action was deciphered by observers.
cards.vis: A detective piecing together random wooden block clues to construct a clear mental picture of what happened at the crime scene.

cards.def: Feeling or expressing remorse or penitence; affected by guilt.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Offer a contrite apology after the argument"
cards.context: Identifies individuals seeking forgiveness or demonstrating genuine regret for their misdeeds.
cards.vis: A guilty puppy weeping big, cinematic tears, bowing its head completely because it can't try to fight the truth that it chewed the couch.

cards.def: Extremely complex and difficult to follow (especially an argument, story, or sentence).
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A convoluted plot line that left viewers confused"
cards.context: Contrasted with clarity, simplicity, or straightforwardness; usually describing bureaucracy, legal jargon, or dense prose.
cards.vis: A thief trying to escape a bank through a wildly twisted, looping, labyrinthine air vent that turns upside down ten times.

cards.def: To yearn to possess or have something, especially something belonging to another.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Covet a colleague's prestigious new promotion"
cards.context: Highlights intense desire, envy, or jealousy, often leading to a character scheming to steal another's property.
cards.vis: A jealous neighbor staring out his window with glowing red eyes at a shiny sports car, plotting to cover it up and steal it.

cards.def: Contemptibly lacking in courage; cowardly.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A craven desertion of duties during a crisis"
cards.context: The absolute opposite of bold or intrepid; used by ETS to harshly criticize characters who flee from conflict.
cards.vis: A soldier diving into a tiny cave, trembling violently and shaking with fear while hiding from a mild thunderstorm outside.

cards.def: Behavior in keeping with good taste and propriety; etiquette.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Maintain strict decorum during the solemn court proceedings"
cards.context: Often mentioned in contexts detailing formal events, royal courts, or high-society gatherings where rules must be followed.
cards.vis: A strict butler perfectly aligning crystal wine glasses using a ruler to ensure absolute, faultless visual order.

cards.def: Neat-handed and skillful in physical movement or mental execution.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A deft manipulation of the musical instrument strings"
cards.context: Used to describe artisans, surgeons, or clever politicians executing precise, masterful work with ease.
cards.vis: A master surgeon completely deaf to loud sirens outside, executing a flawlessly precise, micro-surgical stitch with perfect steady hands.

cards.def: To raise doubts or objections; show reluctance.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Demur at the suggestion of working over the weekend"
cards.context: Signals a character's hesitation, polite refusal, or mild objection to a proposed course of action.
cards.vis: An employee crossing his arms and nervously sweating, asking 'Damn, are you sure?' when his boss tells him to jump into a pool of jello.

cards.def: Imitative of the work of another person, and usually disapproved of for that reason.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "An uninspired, highly derivative pop song"
cards.context: A staple negative GRE descriptor for unoriginal art, literature, or theories copied directly from predecessors.
cards.vis: An artist literally tracing a painting done by his uncle, slapping his own name on it, and pretending he invented it.

cards.def: To remove the moisture from something, typically in order to preserve it; completely dried out.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Desiccate fruit for long-term storage"
cards.context: Appears metaphorically to describe uninspired, lifeless academic prose, or literally in archaeological and botanical descriptions.
cards.vis: A ancient stone statue of a cat sitting under a scorching desert sun, radiating dry heat till the ground around it cracks.

cards.def: A forceful and bitter verbal attack against someone or something.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Launch into a bitter diatribe against institutional corruption"
cards.context: Identifies highly polarized arguments, furious editorials, or aggressive speeches blasting an opponent.
cards.vis: An angry warlord screaming a furious, foaming-at-the-mouth speech, cursing a rival tribe and wishing for their demise.

cards.def: Unwilling or unable to believe something; skeptical.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "An incredulous stare upon hearing the lottery results"
cards.context: Describes characters reacting to absurd claims, miraculous events, or shocking plot twists with utter disbelief.
cards.mnem: In-credit-less (gives no credit to the story).
cards.vis: A cynical detective raising a single eyebrow so high it flies off his head while looking at a suspect claiming an alien ate his homework.

cards.def: Innocent, naive, unsuspecting, and completely without guile.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "An ingenuous child who believes every fairy tale"
cards.context: ETS loves pairing this with words indicating vulnerability or contrasting it sharply with artful, deceptive, disingenuous characters.
cards.vis: An wide-eyed, innocent country girl stepping off a bus into a den of devious con artists, smiling warmly with complete trust.
